description: Regression scenarios for the four-mode stateful-file behavior: first-creation activation prompt, idempotent no-op update, and deprecate-on-contradiction (newest-wins). Mirrors the ywc-review-learnings confirmation-gate contract.

evals.name: create-file-first-run
evals.prompt: Remember this project mission: it is a time-tracking SaaS for Japanese construction SMBs, and the success criterion is that on-site workers finish clock-in/out recording within 30 seconds.
expected_behavior: Mode auto-detects to `update` (user states durable intent, file absent).

Builds an ADD CHANGESET: one Mission/North-Star statement + one measurable Success Criterion (S001, e.g. 'on-site worker completes clock-in within 30s'), each with provenance `brainstorm` and today's date.
Presents the CHANGESET for one confirmation round before writing.

On confirmation, writes docs/project-mission.md with the file skeleton (Mission, Success Criteria table `ID | Criterion | Source | Added | Status`, Out of Scope, Change Log) and sets `<!-- updated: <today> -->`.
Because this is first creation, prints the `@docs/project-mission.md` CLAUDE.md activation prompt exactly once.
anti_behavior: Writing the file without presenting the CHANGESET for confirmation.
Recording a non-measurable success criterion (e.g. 'be fast') instead of an observable done-condition.
Omitting provenance or the `Added` date on any entry.
Skipping the first-creation activation prompt, or printing it on a later non-creating run.

evals.name: idempotent-no-op-update
evals.prompt: Organize the mission again with the same content as before.

preconditions.docs/project-mission.md: exists with identical Mission + S001 already recorded

expected_behavior: Reads the existing file and gathers the candidate from the source.
Classifies every candidate against the file and finds no difference — the confirmed CHANGESET is empty.
Idempotency gate (AC15/NFR3) triggers: makes NO file write, does NOT bump `<!-- updated: DATE -->`, appends NO Change Log row.
anti_behavior: Bumping the `<!-- updated: DATE -->` header on identical content.
Appending a Change Log row for a no-op.
Re-writing the file with reordered-but-equivalent content and calling it a change.

evals.name: deprecate-on-contradiction
evals.prompt: Change the Success Criteria: update the clock-in/out recording target from 30 seconds to a 10-second median.
expected_behavior: Recognizes the new criterion contradicts active S001 (same subject, different target).
Builds a CHANGESET pairing DEPRECATE (S001) with ADD (S002, measurable: 'median clock-in time under 10s', source `plan`, today's date) — newest-wins.
On confirmation, marks S001 `deprecated` with `~~strikethrough~~` and a `> deprecated <today>: superseded by S002` note; never hard-deletes S001.
Bumps `<!-- updated: <today> -->` and appends a Change Log row.
Prints a `Mission updated` confirmation block showing 1 ADD + 1 DEPRECATE.
anti_behavior: Leaving both S001 and S002 active (two contradictory active criteria).
Hard-deleting S001 instead of deprecating it with strikethrough + reason.
Renumbering or reusing the S001 ID for the new entry.
